free_text: Post-neutron mass distribution for U-232(n-th,f)
J.C., G.L., M.A. and experimental site.
Ph. USTHB, B.P. 32 EI Ali, Alger, Algeria
CEN-Grenoble Melusine reactor and
Grenoble high-flux reactor.
15 min and 2 hr irradiations were performed
inside a 1 m3 D2O container near the Melusine reactor
core. The neutron flux was 1.6 x 10**13 n/cm2/sec and
the thermal/epithermal ratio was greater
90 sec irradiations were done in the high-flux
reactor with a neutron flux of 10.**14 n/cm2/sec and
a thermal/epithermal ratio of greater than 5.0x10**3.
.Thermal reactor spectrum.
An energy- and efficiency-calibrated 63 cm3 high
purity germanium detector was used to count and follow
the decay of the different gamma-ray lines to identify
the fission products.
0.04 micrograms of U-232 was placed in a polyethylene
capsule of 7 mm diam. and 15 mm height, covered with
a 2 mm thick layer of special glue. A lid of similar
material was sealed to the top of the capsule to
ensure that no gaseous fission products escape from
the capsule.
.Also blank capsules were used to ensure that no
contaminants from U-235 were present.
.The data were corrected for dead time.
No discussion of errors given.
N.O.
